title: First Solar Q1 Earnings Report Preview: What To Look For
text: Analysts expect First Solar to report strong Q1 2026 results with revenue growth of approximately 22-24% year-over-year. Key metrics to watch include gross margin expansion driven by CURE technology adoption and Section 45X tax credit monetization. The market will be particularly focused on whether the company can sustain the 47% gross margin achieved in Q1 2026 as U.S. manufacturing scales. Module sales volumes and average selling prices by geography will indicate demand strength and pricing power. The company's guidance for Q2 and full-year 2026 will be critical, with particular attention to whether tariff headwinds are worse or better than previously expected. Investors should monitor backlog trends and customer mix shifts. The successful CURE technology launch and ramp represent a significant milestone, with $600 million in potential revenue upside embedded in the backlog through 2028. The South Carolina facility progress toward H2 2026 commercial operations is another key metric, as it will add 3.7 GW capacity. Section 45X tax credit monetization levels will impact earnings quality and cash flow. The company's ability to maintain pricing discipline and customer selectivity in the U.S. market amid policy uncertainty will influence near-term margins. Overall, Q1 results should validate management's 2026 guidance and positioning for long-term growth.
